docs.text: 约 580 个字 预计阅读时间 2 分钟

docs.title: Lesson Abstract
docs.text:
Strucutre of Main paragraph

4 paragraph essay 2 main body paragraphs 5 sentences in each 90 to 100 words each Lesson introduction

[!NOTE] 2 methods for doing a plan Ideas,vocabulary,planning

docs.title: How to write
docs.text: Firstly,Secondly,Finally adv/disadvantages,prob/solution Idea,Explain,Example idea/reason/opinion

docs.title: Firstly,Secondly,Finally
docs.text:
``

You need three individual points

``

Do a three min plan about disagreement

The high school student often immersed in magnificent stress of College entrance examination The work should be assigned to relevant staff,who will be more professional and efficient Teenagers loss no motivation for that 5 sentence:

[!NOTE] There are several reasons why i would argue against having compulsory community service for secondary school students. Firstly,the school curriculum is already full with important academic subjects,such as maths,science and languages.For example,I remember having an extremely busy timetable when I was at high school,and it would not have been possible to add to it.Secondly,students'performance in other subjects would be affected if valuable study time were taken by charity work or neighbourhood improvement schemes. Finally,I believe that teenage students would be reluctant to take part in any programme of obligatory work,and this could lead to poor motivation and even bad behaviour.

Notes:

Linking words don't help your vocabulary score Theme vocabulary is helpful to your score

docs.title: Idea,Explain,Example
docs.text:
``

You need three sociated points

``

Do a three min plan about supporting voluntary part instead of compulsory part

volutary community service is positive Students more motivated if they can choose gain work experience,self confidence,skills 5 sentence:

[!info] On the other hand,the opportunity to do voluntary community service could be extremely positive for high school students.By making these programmes optional,schools would ensure that only motivated students took part.These young people would gain valuable experience in an adult working environment,which could help to build their self confidence and enhance their skills.Having such experience and skills on their CVs could greatly improve school leavers'career prospects.For example,a period of voluntary work experience might impress a university admissions officer or a future employer.

Note

[!tip] The position of example can be changed in your convience.

[!example]

1,

docs.title: Lesson Abstract
docs.text:
2 easy ‘rules’

never write anything new One sentence: repeat summarise Paraphrasing to show varable word Lesson introduction

Paraphrase the answer that you gave in your introduction

docs.title: Example 1 Using high level vocabulary
docs.text:
Introduction It is true that video surveillance has become commonplace in many cities in recent years.While I understand that critics may see this as an invasion of privacy,I believe that there are more benefits than drawbacks.

Conclusion In conclusion,I would argue that the advantages of using video security systems in public places do outweigh the disadvantages.

docs.title: Example 2 disagree = don't believe
docs.text:
Introduction Some people believe that parents of children who attend private schools should not need to contribute to state schools through taxes.Personally,I completely disagree with this view.

Conclusion In conclusion,I do not believe that any financial concessions should be made for people who choose private education.

docs.title: Example 3
docs.text:
Introduction It is true that people in industrialised nations can expect to live longer than ever before.Although there will undoubtedly be some negative consequences of this trend,societies can take steps to mitigate these potential problems.

Conclusion In conclusion,various measures can be taken to tackle the problems that are certain to arise as the populations of countries grow older.

docs.title: 内容规划
docs.text: 后续会在这里更新： - 学习笔记（比如 MkDocs 进阶技巧） - 日常分享（生活/技术类内容） - 联系方式（可以留邮箱/社交账号）

docs.title: 课程指引
docs.text:
约 269 个字 预计阅读时间 1 分钟

这里是所有课程的详细指引，按类别整理好啦～

docs.title: 高等数学（上册）
docs.text: 这门课主要学极限、导数、积分，是理工科基础，重点掌握： - 洛必达法则求极限 - 定积分的几何应用 - 微分方程解法 - 👉 网课推荐：未央指南 # 可加多个链接

docs.title: 线性代数
docs.text: 核心内容是矩阵运算和向量空间，考试常考： 1. 矩阵的逆和秩的计算 2. 特征值和特征向量 3. 二次型标准化

docs.title: 大学物理（力学）
docs.text: 重点章节： - 牛顿三大定律（解题核心） - 功和能的转化 - 刚体的转动

docs.title: 电磁学
docs.text: 难点是电场和磁场的综合应用，建议： - 先吃透高斯定理 - 多做电磁感应的习题

docs.title: 中国传统文化
docs.text: 课程内容： - 儒家思想演变 - 传统节日的由来 - 书法/国画入门

docs.title: 计算机通识
docs.text: 适合零基础，主要讲： - 计算机基本组成 - 简单的Python入门 - 办公软件进阶技巧

docs.title: Python学习
docs.text:
约 49 个字 10 行代码 预计阅读时间不到 1 分钟

这里整理了Python入门核心知识点，附可直接运行的代码示例和避坑提示～

docs.title: 1. 第一个Python程序
docs.text:
# 单行注释：打印Hello World
print("Hello Python!")  

# 多行注释（文档字符串）
"""
演示Python最基础的输出功能
f-string是Python3.6+的格式化方式，推荐使用
"""
username = "新手学习者"
print(f"你好，{username}！欢迎学习Python～")  # 格式化输出

docs.title: TSET
docs.text:
约 257 个字 预计阅读时间 1 分钟

In chip manufacturing, the node names such as "3nm" and "5nm" have long departed from their physical meaning as gate length. Instead, they have become more of an industry convention for marking technological generations. This phenomenon is essentially the result of a combination of technical evolution and commercial promotion. The following explains this from both technical principles and industry logic:

Early process nodes (such as 90nm and 45nm) did directly correspond to the gate length—the key structural size in transistors that controls the flow of current. However, as semiconductor technology entered the era of FinFET (Fin Field-Effect Transistor) and then GAA (Gate-All-Around), a single "gate length" is no longer sufficient to fully measure chip performance.

  • The actual gate length of the 5nm process is approximately 10–12nm, and that of the 3nm process is around 7–8nm, both significantly larger than the nominal value.
  • Physically, when the gate length approaches 1nm, quantum tunneling effects cause electrons to pass through the gate insulator uncontrollably, leading to transistor failure. Current materials and structures cannot easily break this limit. (The diameter of a silicon atom is 0.235nm.)

Today’s "nm‑level" nodes mainly represent transistor density (the number of transistors per square millimeter) and structural innovation, rather than a single physical dimension. There are also definition differences among manufacturers: In the absence of a unified international standard, different companies define "nm nodes" in varying ways: The core metrics such as transistor density and power consumption control for 5nm/3nm processes from TSMC, Samsung, and Intel are not completely consistent. Intel > TSMC > Samsung

docs.title: Speaking part 1 just  warm-up
docs.text: 约 269 个字 预计阅读时间 1 分钟

docs.title: Test abstract
docs.text:
4 to 5 minutes 10 or 11 questions 3 topic areas Don't stop and think,try to show fluency,don't worry about vocabulary or grammar

You can't get a high score from part 1.

docs.title: How to answer
docs.text:
Answer + Reason.Stop with confident slience,smile at the examiner.

Topic often seem childish.

docs.title: Describe a person
docs.text: 3 to 4 miutes 1 minute to prepare speak for 2 minutes task card describe...

docs.title: Before the exam
docs.text: Prepare for common topic areas Vocabulary and ideas are the key

docs.title: Main topic areas:
docs.text: Person,Place,Object Event,Activity,Favourites

docs.title: Structure:Person
docs.text: who (appearance,personality) what he/she does when how where you met why you like this person

docs.title: Preparation tip
docs.text:
think of a 'theme'for any person (friend,family,famous,child etc.) start with easy adjectives search for better words and phrases

Theme is core feature, the adjectives should surround the core feature

Practise workflow:

Choose a topic👉Speak out and record👉 voice-to-word transition tool👉improve the word

Start from the anwers your brain think at frist time, and improve from it, the adoptation needed will be min.

docs.title: Speaking part 2:place
docs.text: a city (you've visited) a city (you would like to visit)somewhere you went on holiday a historic place,river,lake or sea a journey (where you went) a shop,restaurant,street market

docs.title: Structure：Place
docs.text:
Think of a 'theme' for any place start with easy adjectives search for better words and phrases

Fun city 'theme'

Bustling,hectic,thriving,cosmopolitan,multicultural, fascinating,sightseeing,entertainment

You will need to adapt your ideas and improvise in the test.
